title: 「Whether (or not) / If 句」句型
titleZh: 「Whether (or not) / If 句」表達「可否／能否／會否／應否／是否」
titleEn: Using Whether (or not) / If for indirect yes/no questions

source.file: Sentence Sturcture 32 -「Whether (or not) _ If 句」表達「可否 _ 能.pdf
omissions: English versions of Chinese-only teaching explanations and Benefits are faithful editorial translations.
The display title regularises the source heading’s awkward line break as 可否／能否／會否／應否／是否.

formula: Main clause + whether + subject + verb / modal verb + other information

formulas.formula: Main clause + whether + subject + verb / modal verb + other information
Main clause + if + subject + verb / modal verb + other information

example: I’m not sure whether personality is primarily affected by genetics or not.
exampleZh: 我不確定性格是否主要受基因影響。

examples.en: I’m not sure whether personality is primarily affected by genetics or not.
examples.zh: 我不確定性格是否主要受基因影響。
examples.highlight: whether personality is primarily affected by genetics or not

examples.en: I’m not sure if personality is primarily affected by genetics or not.
examples.zh: 我不確定性格是否主要受基因影響。
examples.highlight: if personality is primarily affected by genetics or not

meaning.zh: Whether 和 if 可以引入間接的是非問題，表示「是否」、「會否」或「是不是」，常用於表示不確定、不知道、想知道、詢問、檢查、確認、記得或忘記，以及關心或不在乎。

rules.en: Whether and if have the same meaning in many indirect yes/no questions.
rules.zh: Whether 和 if 在很多間接是非問題中意思相同。
examples: I do not know whether the shop is open.
I do not know if the shop is open.

rules.en: Use statement word order after whether or if: subject + verb, not verb + subject.
rules.zh: Whether / if 後面使用陳述句語序：Subject + verb，而不是 Verb + subject。
examples: I wonder whether he is ready.
I wonder if he is ready.

rules.en: Whether and if can be followed by modal expressions such as can, should, may, will, need to and must.
rules.zh: Whether / if 可以配合 can、should、may、will、need to 和 must 等情態表達。
examples: We must decide whether we should wait.
Please check if visitors may enter.

rules.en: Or not may appear at the end with either whether or if. Whether or not may also stay together, but if or not is generally not used.
rules.zh: Or not 可以放在句末，並與 whether 或 if 搭配。Whether or not 也可以連在一起，但一般不說 if or not。
examples: I do not know whether he will come or not.
I do not know if he will come or not.

rules.en: Whether and if are not interchangeable everywhere. Normally use whether before a to-infinitive, after a preposition, and after verbs such as discuss or consider.
rules.zh: Whether 和 if 並非在所有情況下都可以互換。Whether + to-infinitive、介詞 + whether，以及 discuss / consider + whether 等結構一般應使用 whether。
examples: I cannot decide whether to stay.
It depends on whether the shop is open.
We discussed whether the plan was practical.

rules.en: Do not confuse if meaning ‘whether’ with conditional if meaning ‘if’. Conditional if cannot simply be replaced by whether.
rules.zh: 不要把表示「是否」的 if 與條件句中表示「如果」的 if 混淆；條件句的 if 不能直接改為 whether。
examples: I do not know if he is ready.
If he is ready, we can begin.

rules.en: Common frames that can introduce whether or if include be sure, know, wonder, ask, check, find out, see, remember, forget, confirm, tell someone, care, matter, investigate and test.
rules.zh: 常與 whether 或 if 搭配的句框包括 be sure、know、wonder、ask、check、find out、see、remember、forget、confirm、tell someone、care、matter、investigate 和 test。
examples: Can you tell me whether the library is open?
Please confirm whether the payment has arrived.

rules.en: Formal structures such as decide, discuss, consider, debate, depend on, think about and the question of normally favour whether.
rules.zh: Decide、discuss、consider、debate、depend on、think about、the question of、whether to + verb 和 whether A or B 等搭配通常較適合使用 whether。

benefits.en: It places a direct question inside a complete sentence.
benefits.zh: 把直接問題放進完整句子，讓你在同一句中說明自己不知道、想知道或正在確認甚麼。
清楚表達不確定情況，讓讀者知道答案可能是「是」，也可能是「否」。

benefits.en: It connects a question with a person’s thought or action.
benefits.zh: 幫助連接「一個問題」和「某人的想法或行動」，避免使用兩個分開的短句。

benefits.en: It makes speaking and writing smoother through reusable sentence frames.
benefits.zh: 讓說話和寫作更流暢；掌握 I’m not sure whether...、I don’t know if... 等句框後，不需要每次從零開始組織句子。

benefits.en: Whether can make formal expression clearer.
benefits.zh: Whether 可以令正式表達更清楚，適合報告、會議和正式電郵。

benefits.en: If provides a natural everyday alternative.
benefits.zh: If 提供自然的日常說法，在一般對話中語氣自然直接。

sourceOmissions: The PDF presents many Important Rules and all Benefits explanations mainly in Chinese; their English versions are faithful editorial translations.
The display title regularises the source heading’s awkward line break as 可否／能否／會否／應否／是否.

en: Rewrite each sentence as an indirect question in two ways.
When the prompt contains either way, include or not in both answers.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 將每題改成兩種間接問句。
如題目包含 either way，兩個答案均加入 or not。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。
